title: Partition Function Inflation: Why Hashed N-Gram Caches Produce Invalid BPB Scores (Non-Record, Analytical)
author: Robby955

data.description: Analytical diagnosis of cache-augmented Dirichlet-multinomial scoring showing partition function inflation and invalid BPB from unnormalized probability mass.
Partition function measurement diagnostic during evaluation via environment variables.

novel_contributions: Derived a closed-form partition function inflation result for recursive Dirichlet-multinomial cache scoring.
Showed that hashed n-gram caches can produce dramatically improved but invalid BPB scores due to unnormalized probability distributions.
Introduced a normalization sweep demonstrating that apparent cache gains disappear under proper normalization.
Provided evidence that random collision partners and synthetic fake-count floors reproduce the observed BPB gap, indicating inflation rather than linguistic signal.
Defined a Z diagnostic for detecting unreliable unnormalized cache scores.
